evidence_reviewed.summary: Review package froze PSPC as disabled, audit-only, mainline-disconnected, unregistered, and forbidden from runtime authority.
Fixture-boundary harness rejected missing or false forbidden flags, enabled=true, mainline_connected=true, and runtime-authority fields.
Default-off observer stayed enabled=false, mainline_connected=false, runtime_authority=none, unregistered, audit-only, and non-executable.
Recorded replay preserved response hashes, snapshot hashes, memory, approval, gate, and runtime-output state while PSPC added only audit artifacts.
Deterministic no-live-user smoke preserved runtime behavior hashes, confirmed no runtime import or registry offenders, and recorded no side effects.

remaining_gaps: No runtime-adjacent hook insertion Stage Card exists yet.
No proof exists that a future disabled hook can be imported near runtime without accidental enablement.
No proof exists that runtime output remains unchanged when a hook module is physically present in a runtime-adjacent package.
No live user, memory, gate, approval, transport, or proactive-channel safety evidence exists for PSPC.
what_it_proves: Prior PSPC shadow evidence is sufficient to write a future separate disabled runtime shadow hook Stage Card only.
